description: Glucose < 70 mg/dL represents immediate hypoglycemia risk.
clinicalAction: Consume 15g fast-acting carbohydrates immediately (e.g., half a cup of fruit juice, 3-4 teaspoons of sugar/honey). Recheck blood sugar in 15 minutes. Contact your care provider.
patientMessageTemplate: ⚠️ Low Sugar Alert: Your glucose is low at {value} mg/dL. Please eat 3 teaspoons of sugar or half a cup of juice immediately and rest.

description: Glucose > 300 mg/dL represents acute hyperglycemic crisis risk.
clinicalAction: Contact doctor or emergency services immediately. Check for urine ketones. Avoid physical activity.
patientMessageTemplate: ⚠️ Critical High Alert: Your glucose is very high at {value} mg/dL. Please contact your doctor immediately and avoid physical exertion.

description: Fasting glucose is above type-specific target limits for 3 consecutive fasting logs.
clinicalAction: Review bedtime snack composition and check medication adherence. Recommend consulting endocrinologist for potential dosage adjustments.
patientMessageTemplate: We noticed your fasting glucose has been high for 3 consecutive days. This trend can indicate a need to adjust your evening meals or medication. Please share this log with your doctor.

description: Fasting glucose is above the maximum target for the patient's diabetes type.
clinicalAction: Schedule HbA1c test and review medication/basal insulin with doctor.
patientMessageTemplate: Your fasting glucose is elevated. Avoid heavy carbohydrate meals late in the evening and discuss this trend with your care team.

description: Post-meal glucose is above type-specific targets, representing elevated post-prandial glucose.
clinicalAction: Review portion sizes of carbohydrates (rice, roti). Walk for 15-20 minutes post-meal.
patientMessageTemplate: Your blood sugar rose to {value} mg/dL after your meal. A quick 15-minute walk now will help lower this spike naturally!

description: Three consecutive normal readings (between 70 and 140 mg/dL).
clinicalAction: Continue positive habits. Reinforce patient compliance.
patientMessageTemplate: Fantastic job! Your last 3 readings have all been within your target range. Consistency is the key to managing your diabetes.

description: Glucose > 140 mg/dL with low physical activity level.
clinicalAction: Recommend light aerobic activity (walking) to enhance insulin sensitivity.
patientMessageTemplate: Your blood sugar is {value} mg/dL and your activity level is low. Moving around for just 10-15 minutes can significantly improve your body's glucose response.

description: Meal likely has high glycemic impact based on content analysis.
clinicalAction: Review meal composition and portion size.
patientMessageTemplate: Your recent meal may have contributed significantly to your glucose increase.

description: Fasting glucose between 70 and 100 mg/dL is optimal.
clinicalAction: Continue current diet, activity, and medication plan.
patientMessageTemplate: Excellent fasting glucose of {value} mg/dL! You are starting your day in the target zone. Keep up the great work.

description: Post-meal glucose between 70 and 140 mg/dL is optimal.
clinicalAction: Maintain current meal planning and post-meal activity levels.
patientMessageTemplate: Great post-meal glucose of {value} mg/dL! Your body processed this meal beautifully. Keep logging your meals.

description: Random glucose between 140 and 200 mg/dL is slightly elevated.
clinicalAction: Monitor food intake, stay active, and review trends over the next 2 days.
patientMessageTemplate: Your glucose reading is slightly elevated at {value} mg/dL. Try drinking some water and doing a light stretch or walk.

description: High glycemic variability detected using statistical Coefficient of Variation (>36%), indicating unstable glucose control.
clinicalAction: Review glucose logs and medication adherence.
patientMessageTemplate: Your glucose levels have been fluctuating significantly. Consider discussing your recent diet and medication schedule with your doctor.

description: Glucose is increasing rapidly over a short period.
clinicalAction: Monitor glucose closely and review recent meals.
patientMessageTemplate: Your glucose is rising rapidly. Stay hydrated and monitor your readings closely.

description: Glucose is decreasing rapidly and may lead to hypoglycemia.
clinicalAction: Consider checking glucose more frequently and keeping fast-acting carbohydrates available.
patientMessageTemplate: Your glucose is dropping quickly. Please monitor closely.

description: Elevated glucose combined with repeated medication non-adherence.
clinicalAction: Review medication adherence patterns and address potential barriers.
patientMessageTemplate: Your glucose is elevated and medication may have been missed.

description: Multiple instances of low blood sugar (<70 mg/dL) captured across recent history windows.
clinicalAction: Urgent review of basal insulin or sulfonylurea dosages. High risk for severe clinical events or hypoglycemia unawareness.
patientMessageTemplate: ⚠️ Alert: We noticed recurrent low blood sugar readings. Please verify your insulin/medication logs and coordinate an adjustment with your care team.

description: Consecutive post-meal readings exceed diabetes type specific bounds.
clinicalAction: Evaluate meal insulin-to-carbohydrate ratios or post-prandial medication adherence.
patientMessageTemplate: Your blood sugar has spiked after your last few meals. Consider logging precise portions and adjusting your pre-meal planning.
